description:
Follow-up to fz-ul4.29.5. Today `parking.rs:113` excludes
`used_as_closure_target` fns from `natively_callable` because the
old runtime helper `fz_closure_invoke` produced a uniform-ABI callee
frame — the body had to consume that frame, so it stayed uniform.

With .29.5's stub design, the stub IS the ABI adapter. A stub can
load captures from the closure object kind-aware, marshal them into
native ABI registers / params, and synchronously call (or `return_call`
for tail) a native body. The closure-target body becomes free to use
the native ABI like any directly-called native fn.

## Scope

- Drop the `used_as_closure_target` exclusion at `parking.rs:113`.
- Stub generation: when the resolved narrow spec is native, emit a
  direct `call` / `return_call` rather than allocating + returning a
  frame. The stub then returns the native body's tagged FzValue
  return through the cont (uniform caller) or directly (native caller).
- Add fixture: closure target with a body that benefits from native
  ABI (e.g., int-int arithmetic recursive helper invoked through a
  closure). Acceptance: `expect_clif_excludes: fz_alloc_frame_dyn`
  for the stub.

## Why deferred from .29.5

.29.5 already collapses three concerns. Adding the native-eligibility
lift mixes a perf optimization into a correctness-and-shape rework.
Separation keeps the .29.5 commit reviewable.

## Blocked by

fz-ul4.29.5.
title: .29.5.1: Lift closure-target native-eligibility
